title: CLI command with flexible identifier resolution
description:
As a Plexus user
I want to run optimization from the CLI with any identifier type
So that I can use names, keys, IDs, or external IDs interchangeably

## Acceptance Criteria

- CLI command: plexus procedure optimize
- Accepts scorecard and score parameters
- Resolves identifiers flexibly (name/key/ID/externalID)
- Supports configuration parameters (days, max_iterations, etc.)
- Provides clear output formats (table/JSON/YAML)

## Gherkin Specification

```gherkin
Feature: CLI Command with Flexible Identifier Resolution
  As a Plexus user
  I want to optimize scores from the command line
  So that I can easily run optimizations without complex setup

Scenario: Run optimization with score names
  Given I have a scorecard named "Customer Service"
  And it has a score named "Empathy Check"
  When I run: plexus procedure optimize -s "Customer Service" -c "Empathy Check"
  Then the scorecard identifier resolves to its ID
  And the score identifier resolves to its ID
  And the optimization runs successfully

Scenario: Run optimization with keys
  Given I have a scorecard with key "customer-service"
  And it has a score with key "empathy-check"
  When I run: plexus procedure optimize -s customer-service -c empathy-check
  Then the identifiers resolve correctly
  And the optimization runs successfully

Scenario: Run optimization with IDs
  Given I have a scorecard with ID "sc-abc123"
  And it has a score with ID "score-def456"
  When I run: plexus procedure optimize -s sc-abc123 -c score-def456
  Then the identifiers resolve directly (no lookup needed)
  And the optimization runs successfully

Scenario: Run optimization with external IDs
  Given I have a scorecard with externalId "EXTERNAL-CS-001"
  And it has a score with externalId "EMP-SCORE-42"
  When I run: plexus procedure optimize -s EXTERNAL-CS-001 -c EMP-SCORE-42
  Then the identifiers resolve via external ID lookup
  And the optimization runs successfully

Scenario: Configure optimization parameters
  Given a valid scorecard and score
  When I run: plexus procedure optimize -s test-sc -c test-score --days 60 --max-iterations 3 --improvement-threshold 0.05
  Then the optimizer uses a 60-day feedback window
  And runs maximum 3 iterations
  And requires 0.05 minimum AC1 improvement to continue

Scenario: Dry run mode
  Given a valid scorecard and score
  When I run: plexus procedure optimize -s test-sc -c test-score --dry-run
  Then the baseline evaluation runs
  And the analyzer proposes changes
  But no score versions are created
  And no HITL gates appear

Scenario: Output format options
  Given a completed optimization
  When I run with --output json
  Then the results are formatted as JSON
  When I run with --output yaml
  Then the results are formatted as YAML
  When I run with --output table
  Then the results are formatted as a readable table
```
